description: South African enterprise software patterns, business rules, and conventions. Injected into system prompts to ensure generated code is SA-specific and production-ready.

entries.title: South African VAT Calculation
entries.content:
South African VAT rules:
- Standard VAT rate: 15% (effective 1 April 2018)
- VAT registration threshold: R1,000,000 turnover in 12 months
- VAT calculation: vat_amount = line_total * 0.15 where line_total = quantity * unit_price_excl_vat
- Total inclusive: total_incl_vat = line_total + vat_amount
- Zero-rated items: basic foodstuffs (brown bread, maize meal, rice, vegetables, fruit, vegetable oil, milk, eggs), petrol/diesel, exports, international transport
- Exempt items: financial services, residential rental, public transport, educational services
- VAT invoice requirements: seller VAT number, buyer VAT number (if registered), invoice date, description of goods/services, quantity, price excl VAT, VAT amount, total incl VAT
- Tax period: usually bi-monthly (Category A) or monthly (Category B for turnover > R30M)
- SARS eFiling integration: VAT201 return submission
entries.code_pattern:
# Python VAT calculation
def calculate_vat(quantity: int, unit_price: float, vat_rate: float = 0.15) -> dict:
    line_total = quantity * unit_price
    vat_amount = round(line_total * vat_rate, 2)
    total_incl = round(line_total + vat_amount, 2)
    return {
        'line_total_excl_vat': round(line_total, 2),
        'vat_amount': vat_amount,
        'vat_rate': vat_rate,
        'total_incl_vat': total_incl
    }

entries.title: South African Rand (ZAR) Formatting
entries.content:
Currency formatting rules for South African Rand:
- ISO code: ZAR
- Symbol: R (placed before amount, no space: R1,234.56)
- Decimal separator: . (period)
- Thousands separator: , (comma) or space
- Standard format: R 1,234.56
- For amounts > R100, cents are optional: R 1,235
- Negative amounts: -R 1,234.56 or (R 1,234.56)
- Always store amounts as integers (cents) in database to avoid floating point issues
- Display conversion: amount_display = f'R {amount_cents / 100:,.2f}'

entries.title: B-BBEE (Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment)
entries.content:
B-BBEE compliance requirements for enterprise software:
- B-BBEE Level: 1-8 (Level 1 is best, 135% recognition; Level 8 is worst, 10% recognition)
- Non-compliant: 0% recognition (effectively excluded from government and large corporate tenders)
- Five elements scored: Ownership (25 points), Management Control (19 points), Skills Development (20 points + 5 bonus), Enterprise & Supplier Development (40 points + 4 bonus), Socio-Economic Development (5 points)
- Generic scorecard applies to companies with turnover > R50M
- QSE (Qualifying Small Enterprise) scorecard: turnover R10M-R50M
- EME (Exempted Micro Enterprise): turnover < R10M, automatically Level 4 (or Level 1 if >51% Black ownership)
- B-BBEE certificate must be verified by a SANAS-accredited verification agency
- Required fields in supplier/customer models: bbbee_level (1-8 or null), bbbee_certificate_number, bbbee_expiry_date, bbbee_verification_agency, black_ownership_percentage, black_women_ownership_percentage

entries.title: POPIA (Protection of Personal Information Act)
entries.content:
POPIA compliance requirements (South Africa's GDPR equivalent):
- Effective: 1 July 2021, enforcement from 1 July 2022
- Regulator: Information Regulator (South Africa)
- Key requirements for software:
  1. Consent: Must capture explicit consent before processing personal info. Fields: consent_given (bool), consent_date (datetime), consent_method (string)
  2. Purpose limitation: Only collect data for stated purpose. Document purpose in privacy policy.
  3. Data minimization: Only collect necessary fields. No 'nice to have' personal data.
  4. Retention: Define and enforce data retention periods. Auto-delete after period expires.
  5. Right to access: Users can request all their data. Build data export endpoint.
  6. Right to deletion: Users can request deletion. Build soft-delete + hard-delete endpoints.
  7. Right to correction: Users can update their info. Build profile edit endpoints.
  8. Breach notification: Must notify regulator and affected persons within 72 hours.
  9. Cross-border transfer: Personal info cannot leave SA without adequate protection.
  10. Information Officer: Company must appoint and register an Information Officer.
- Required fields on Person/Customer models: popia_consent (bool), popia_consent_date (datetime), popia_consent_method (enum: 'website_form', 'email', 'written', 'verbal'), data_retention_expiry (date), marketing_consent (bool)
entries.code_pattern:
# SQLAlchemy POPIA compliance fields
class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'
    # ... other fields
    popia_consent = Column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False)
    popia_consent_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    popia_consent_method = Column(String(20), nullable=True)  # website_form, email, written, verbal
    marketing_consent = Column(Boolean, default=False)
    data_retention_expiry = Column(Date, nullable=True)
    
    def check_popia_compliance(self) -> bool:
        if not self.popia_consent:
            return False
        if self.data_retention_expiry and self.data_retention_expiry < date.today():
            return False
        return True

entries.title: Authentication & Security Best Practices
entries.content:
MANDATORY security patterns for all generated code:
1. Password hashing: ALWAYS use bcrypt via passlib. NEVER store plaintext passwords.
2. JWT tokens: Set access_token expiry to 30 minutes. Set refresh_token expiry to 7 days. Use HS256 algorithm minimum.
3. CORS: Always configure CORS middleware. Use specific origins in production (not '*'). Allow credentials.
4. Rate limiting: Add rate limiting on auth endpoints (max 5 login attempts per minute per IP).
5. Input validation: Use Pydantic models for ALL request bodies. Validate email format, password strength (min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number).
6. SQL injection: Use ORM (SQLAlchemy/Drizzle) for all queries. Never use raw SQL with user input.
7. Error messages: Never expose internal details (stack traces, DB errors) to clients. Return generic messages.
8. HTTPS: Document that production must use HTTPS.
9. Secrets: Use environment variables for all secrets. Never hardcode API keys, DB passwords, JWT secrets.
10. Audit trail: Log all authentication events (login, logout, failed attempts, password changes) with timestamp and IP.

entries.title: FastAPI Production Patterns
entries.content:
MANDATORY patterns for all FastAPI code generation:
1. Timestamps: Add created_at (DateTime, default=utcnow) and updated_at (DateTime, onupdate=utcnow) on ALL models.
2. Soft delete: Add is_active (Boolean, default=True) on all models. Filter by is_active=True in all queries.
3. Pagination: All list endpoints accept skip (int, default=0) and limit (int, default=20, max=100).
4. Response models: Create separate Pydantic response models. Never return ORM objects directly.
5. Error handling: Wrap all DB operations in try/except. Return HTTPException with appropriate status codes.
6. CORS: Add CORSMiddleware to app with configurable origins.
7. Health check: Add GET /health endpoint returning {'status': 'healthy', 'version': '1.0.0'}.
8. OpenAPI tags: Group endpoints with tags for better documentation.
9. Dependencies: Use FastAPI Depends() for auth, DB sessions, pagination.
10. Logging: Use Python logging module. Log all errors and important business events.
11. Environment: Use pydantic-settings for configuration from .env file.
12. UUID primary keys: Use UUID4 for public-facing IDs. Keep integer IDs for internal foreign keys only if needed for performance.
entries.code_pattern:
# FastAPI boilerplate with all patterns
from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from datetime import datetime
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

app = FastAPI(title='Pablo Generated API', version='1.0.0')
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=os.getenv('CORS_ORIGINS', 'http://localhost:3000').split(','),
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=['*'],
    allow_headers=['*'],
)

@app.get('/health', tags=['system'])
def health_check():
    return {'status': 'healthy', 'version': '1.0.0', 'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat()}

# Pagination dependency
def pagination(skip: int = Query(0, ge=0), limit: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100)):
    return {'skip': skip, 'limit': limit}

entries.content:
When generating seed or demo data for South African systems, use these realistic examples instead of generic 'John Doe' data:

Phone format: +27 XX XXX XXXX (drop leading 0 for international)
ID numbers: 13 digits, format YYMMDDGSSSCAZ (gender at position 7-10, citizenship at 11)

entries.title: Sales Pipeline & Commission Calculation
entries.content:
Standard sales pipeline stages and commission rules for SA enterprise:

Pipeline stages (in order):
1. lead_qualified — Initial contact, need confirmed
2. discovery — Requirements gathering, solution mapping
3. proposal — Formal proposal/quote sent
4. negotiation — Terms discussion, pricing adjustments
5. verbal_agreement — Client verbally agrees
6. contract_sent — Legal documents sent for signature
7. closed_won — Deal signed, revenue recognized
8. closed_lost — Deal lost (must capture lost_reason)

Commission rules:
- Standard commission: 5% of deal value on closed_won
- Tiered commission: 5% up to R500K, 7% R500K-R2M, 10% above R2M
- Commission is calculated on the deal value excl. VAT
- Commission is payable after client payment received (not on deal close)
- Clawback: if client cancels within 90 days, commission is reversed

Deal fields: deal_name, deal_value_excl_vat, deal_stage, expected_close_date, probability (auto from stage), assigned_rep_id, customer_id, lost_reason (required if closed_lost), commission_amount (auto-calculated), commission_paid (bool), commission_paid_date

entries.title: South African Payment & Banking Integrations
entries.content:
Common payment integrations for SA enterprise software:

PayFast (most popular):
- Sandbox URL: https://sandbox.payfast.co.za/eng/process
- Production URL: https://www.payfast.co.za/eng/process
- Required fields: merchant_id, merchant_key, amount, item_name
- Webhook (ITN): POST to your notify_url with payment confirmation
- Supported: credit card, instant EFT, SnapScan, Mobicred, Masterpass

Ozow (instant EFT):
- API: https://api.ozow.com
- Supports: instant EFT from all major SA banks
- Webhook: POST with transaction status

Yoco (card payments):
- API: https://online.yoco.com/v1/charges
- POS integration available
- Supports: Visa, Mastercard, Amex

Bank integration (for ERP):
- FNB: Business Online API, host-to-host payments
- Standard Bank: BOL (Business Online) API
- Absa: CashFocus host-to-host
- Nedbank: NetBank Business API
- All support: EFT payments, bank statements (MT940/CSV), real-time notifications

SARS integration:
- eFiling API for tax submissions (VAT201, IT12, EMP201)
- SARS e@syFile for payroll submissions
- Tax certificates: IRP5 (employee), IT3(b) (investment), IT3(c) (contracts)

entries.title: South African Solar/Renewable Energy Patterns
entries.content:
Solar PPA and energy software patterns for South Africa:

NERSA (National Energy Regulator of SA):
- Registration required for generation > 1MW
- Small Scale Embedded Generation (SSEG) rules for < 1MW
- Wheeling: energy transmitted via Eskom/municipal grid from generator to off-taker
- Wheeling charges: use-of-system charge + losses + admin fee

PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) terms:
- Typical duration: 10-25 years
- Escalation: CPI-linked (typically CPI - 1% or fixed 5-7% per annum)
- Tariff structure: R/kWh (cents per kilowatt-hour), typically R0.80-R1.50/kWh
- Comparison: Eskom tariff ~R2.50/kWh (2024/25), municipalities add 20-40% markup
- Savings: 20-40% vs grid tariff

Carbon credits:
- Gold Standard or Verra (VCS) certification
- 1 carbon credit = 1 tonne CO2 equivalent avoided
- SA grid emission factor: ~1.06 tCO2/MWh
- Price: $5-15/tCO2e (voluntary market)

Common models:
- Site: location, capacity_kw, panel_type, inverter_type, commissioned_date
- Generation: site_id, date, kwh_generated, kwh_exported, kwh_consumed
- Invoice: ppa_id, period_start, period_end, kwh_delivered, tariff_rate, amount_excl_vat, vat, total
- CarbonCredit: site_id, vintage_year, tonnes_co2, verification_standard, serial_number, status (issued/retired/sold)

entries.title: SAP Integration Patterns
entries.content:
Common SAP integration patterns for South African enterprises:

BAPI calls (synchronous):
- BAPI_MATERIAL_GETLIST — Retrieve material master data
- BAPI_CUSTOMER_GETLIST — Retrieve customer master data
- BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 — Create sales order
- BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST — Post accounting document
- Always call BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after successful BAPI calls

IDoc processing (asynchronous):
- ORDERS05 — Sales order
- DEBMAS07 — Customer master
- MATMAS05 — Material master
- INVOIC02 — Invoice
- Direction: 1 = outbound (SAP to external), 2 = inbound (external to SAP)

RFC connection:
- Use pyrfc library for Python → SAP connectivity
- Connection params: ashost, sysnr, client, user, passwd, lang
- Always handle ABAPApplicationError and ABAPRuntimeError

OData (S/4HANA):
- Base URL: https://<host>/sap/opu/odata/sap/
- Common services: API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, API_SALES_ORDER_SRV, API_MATERIAL_STOCK_SRV
- Authentication: Basic auth or OAuth2 client credentials
- Pagination: $top, $skip, $count
- Filtering: $filter with OData operators (eq, ne, gt, lt, and, or)

BTP (Business Technology Platform):
- CAP (Cloud Application Programming) model for extensions
- Destination service for connection management
- XSUAA for auth (OAuth2 + JWT)
